# Key Screen Review

The `home` surface was chosen because it establishes the public catalogue mental model, primary selection hierarchy, and the loading treatment reused by the rest of the flow. The reviewed specimen uses the approved calm-catalogue direction at mobile and desktop widths with the same fixture content.

The mobile version keeps the item list and primary action in one readable column. The desktop version widens the catalogue without changing information order or inventing new controls. In both versions, `home-loading` preserves the content silhouette, exposes visible focus, and avoids a blocking full-page spinner. The user approved these two responsive views as the quality baseline for broader system work; no key-screen blockers remained.
